summary: Europe's largest economy and de facto leader of the EU, built on an export-driven industrial model and a post-WWII commitment to multilateralism — now reckoning with energy dependence on Russia and rising defence demands.

history.detail: Bismarck unifies German states into the German Empire under Prussia.
Germany leads the Central Powers; defeated 1918, punished by Versailles.
NSDAP takes control; rapid militarisation begins.
Germany invades Poland; Holocaust kills ~6 million Jews.
Allied occupation splits Germany; Berlin Wall built 1961.
West Germany rearmed and integrated into Western alliance.
Co-founds European Economic Community with France.
Communist East Germany collapses; reunification follows 1990.
Germany gives up the Deutschmark for the common European currency.
Germany leads EU austerity response; tensions with southern Europe.
Merkel accepts ~1M refugees; political backlash fuels AfD rise.
Russia's Ukraine invasion triggers Germany's historic defence policy reversal.

finance.currency_role: EUR — Germany is the euro-zone's anchor economy
finance.central_bank: Deutsche Bundesbank (1957) — most influential in ECB policy

tech_startups.vc_ecosystem: Europe's third largest; strong industrial tech and fintech

resources.oil_gas: Almost none; historically dependent on Russian gas (Nord Stream)
resources.coal: Large lignite (brown coal) reserves; phasing out by 2030
resources.agriculture: Major producer; wheat, sugar beet, pork

follow_the_money.detail: Billions invested in Russian gas infrastructure; sabotaged 2022

theories_disputed.detail: Pipelines blown up Sept 2022; US, Russia, and Ukraine all suspected by different investigations.
Bundestag inquiry confirmed BND shared intelligence with NSA illegally.

connections.detail: Franco-German axis drives EU; Élysée Treaty since 1963
NATO member; 35,000 US troops stationed in Germany
Sanctions post-Ukraine; former major gas customer
Largest trading partner; Volkswagen, BMW have major China exposure
